title: centaurea sterilis subsp. semiusta
intro: Centaurea sterilis subsp. semiusta is a sub-species of the Centaurea sterilis plant, commonly known as the Cornflower or Corn Marigold, which is a flowering plant in the daisy family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea sterilis subsp. semiusta include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of various skin conditions such as wounds and burns. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
